data.title: American Tower Q1 earnings top estimates, guidance raised on robust demand for infrastructure
data.description:
American Tower (AMT) stock rose 1.8% in Tuesday premarket trading after the telecommunications tower REIT posted stronger-than-expected top and bottom lines, bolstered by robust demand for communications infrastructure.

The company increased its 2026 guidance to reflect estimated positive foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations and accelerating straight-line revenue in Latin America.

The REIT now expects 2026 adjusted FFO per share of $10.90-$11.07 (midpoint $10.99 vs. Visible Alpha consensus of $10.98) compared with its prior guidance of $10.78-$10.95. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be $7.195B-$7.265B (midpoint $7.23B vs. $7.15B Visible Alpha estimate), up from its previous outlook of $7.09B-$7.16B. Its guidance for total property revenue increased to $10.585B-$10.735B (midpoint $10.66B vs. $10.548B Visible Alpha estimate) from its previous view of $10.44B-$10.59BN. 

Q1 AFFO per share of $2.84, beating the Visible Alpha consensus of $2.75, rose from $2.63 in Q3 and $2.75 in Q4 2024.

Total operating revenue of $2.74B, topping the $2.65B consensus, was flat with the previous quarter and increased from  $2.56B a year ago.

Total property revenue of $2.67B grew 6.8% Y/Y, with tenant billings rising 2.3% Y/Y. Property gross margin improved to 75.1% from 74.7% in Q4 2025.

"The structural growth drivers of our business continue to strengthen, with rising mobile data consumption, accelerating cloud adoption, and the rapid expansion of AI-driven workloads all pointing toward sustained investment in high-quality digital infrastructure," said CEO Steve Vondran.

Total operating expenses of $1.50B, vs. the Visible Alpha estimate of $1.46B, dropped from $1.58B in Q4 and climbed from $1.31B in Q1 2025.

Adjusted EBITDA of $1.84B, exceeding the $1.77B VA estimate, fell from $1.82B in the prior quarter and grew from $1.74B in the year-ago period.

Conference call at 8:30 AM ET.

data.title: Silicon Motion Technology likely to report 'solid' Q1, Wedbush says
data.description:
Silicon Motion Technology (SIMO) is likely to report “solid” first-quarter results and reiterate its full-year expectations, Wedbush Securities said. 

“As a reminder, SIMO management tends towards conservatism when guiding, creating probability of upside should the quarter proceed as expected,” analyst Matt Bryson wrote in a note to clients. “With Chinese handset vendors necessarily turning to domestic supply, we anticipate SIMO should meaningfully grow its eMMC/UFS controller business, with strength driven by continued [Yangtze Memory Technologies/ChangXin Memory Technologies] bit growth. While we believe retail demand (a point of strength for SIMO historically) has dipped as higher memory prices have led demand destruction, we believe the impact has likely been somewhat moderated by module vendors gaining incremental share at PC OEMs as even tier one vendors have been scrambling to ensure supply. We expect Silicon Motion should continue to see some benefit from share gains at fabs (particularly with its high performance gen 5 PCIe controllers).”

Bryson reiterated his Outperform rating on Silicon Motion but upped his price target to $180 from $150.

Turning to guidance, Silicon Motion should continue to get a tailwind from Yangtze Memory Technologies. The Chinese memory maker has continued to enable Chinese smartphone builds, even if growth is not as strong as expected going into the year, Bryson added.

“Higher bit ASPs could continue to boost revenues (albeit we would not be surprised if module GMs fall somewhat). SIMO should continue to benefit from newer client SSD controller socket wins (both 4 and 8 channel PCIe designs). Net, our best guess is SIMO guides Q2 at least in-line and reiterates its outlook for the full year.”

data.title: Corning falls despite Q1 beat as Q2 sales outlook below estimates
data.description:
Shares of Corning (GLW) fell about 10% premarket on Tuesday despite first-quarter results beating estimates.

However, the second quarter's sales guidance of $4.6B came in below analysts' estimate of  $4.67B.

Q1 Metrics

Core sales jumped 18% year-over-year to about $4.35B. Core EPS surged around 30% year-over-year to $0.70. Both top and bottom line numbers beat analysts' expectations.

"Additionally, we finalized two more hyperscaler deals similar in size and duration to our recently announced multiyear, up-to-$6 billion agreement with Meta. In total, we have powerful momentum across our Market-Access Platforms," said Chairman, President and CEO Wendell Weeks. "Based on strong demand for our innovations, we plan to upgrade and extend our Springboard plan through 2030 at our May 6 investor event. We will introduce our new Photonics Market-Access Platform and explain the underlying technical and demand trends driving our Gen AI product development."

First quarter net sales from the Optical Communications unit soared 36% year-over-year to around $1.85B.

The company created a Glass Innovations segment, combining its former Display and Specialty Materials segments. Sales from this unit grew 1% year-over-year to $1.42B.

Net sales from its Automotive unit fell 1% year-over-year to $437M. Meanwhile, net sales from the Solar unit surged 80% year-over-year to $370M.

Outlook

Corning said that for the second quarter, management expects to grow core sales about 14% year over year to about $4.6B, versus a consensus estimate of $4.67B.

The company said the second-quarter guidance includes an extended maintenance shutdown at Corning’s solar wafer facility, including the transition to a permanent power system while the company repairs, upgrades, and modifies production equipment to increase throughput in future quarters. This will cause an additional $30M of expense in the second quarter versus the first quarter and is included in guidance, the company added.

Corning expects core EPS to grow about 25% year over year to a range of $0.73 to $0.77 (midpoint at $0.75) versus a consensus estimate of $0.75.

 More on Corning

data.title: Evercore leads all large-cap financial stocks in YoY CapEx growth
data.description:
Capital expenditure growth is a key indicator of how aggressively a company is investing in its future, whether through new infrastructure, equipment, technology, or capacity expansion.

The following list ranks U.S. large-cap financials stocks by their year-over-year CapEx growth percentage, offering a data-driven look at which companies are putting the most capital to work relative to the prior year.

Below is a list of 10 U.S. financials stocks ranked according to their year-over-year capital expenditure growth percentage. The list spans multiple sub-sectors within financials, including Investment Banking and Brokerage, Life and Health Insurance, Asset Management and Custody Banks, Consumer Finance, Property and Casualty Insurance, Transaction & Payment Processing Services, and Financial Exchanges and Data.

Evercore (EVR) tops the list with a CapEx growth rate of 145.85% year-over-year, investing $74 million in capital expenditures in fiscal 2025, a 145.8% jump from the prior year, as the independent investment bank expanded its office infrastructure and technology platforms off the back of a record $3.9 billion in revenue, itself up 29.4% year-over-year.

Globe Life (GL) and Blackstone (BX) follow in second and third place with growth rates of 100.55% and 88.41% respectively, while American Express (AXP) and W. R. Berkley (WRB) round out the top five at 61.28% and 60.94%.

Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) and S&P Global (SPGI) occupy the sixth and seventh positions with CapEx growth of 58.76% and 57.26%, while SoFi Technologies (SOFI) and BlackRock (BLK) follow at 57.16% and 47.06%.

Principal Financial Group (PFG) rounds out the list at 42.44%, having delivered 13% non-GAAP EPS growth and $1.81 trillion in assets under administration in fiscal 2025.

Globe Life (GL) is the sole Strong Buy at 4.57 according to Seeking Alpha Quant ratings, the only such rating across the entire screen, while Blackstone (BX), FIS, and S&P Global (SPGI) all carry Sell ratings at 2.24, 2.30, and 2.25 respectively, creating an unusual dynamic where three of the list's heaviest-spending names are also the quant model's least-favored picks.

Here is the list:

  1. Evercore Inc. (EVR), CapEx growth YoY: 145.85%

  2. Globe Life Inc. (GL), CapEx growth YoY: 100.55%

  3. Blackstone Inc. (BX), CapEx growth YoY: 88.41%

  4. American Express Company (AXP), CapEx growth YoY: 61.28%

  5. W. R. Berkley Corporation (WRB), CapEx growth YoY: 60.94%

  6. Fidelity National Information Services, Inc. (FIS), CapEx growth YoY: 58.76%

  7. S&P Global Inc. (SPGI), CapEx growth YoY: 57.26%

  8. SoFi Technologies, Inc. (SOFI), CapEx growth YoY: 57.16%

  9. BlackRock, Inc. (BLK), CapEx growth YoY: 47.06%

  10. Principal Financial Group, Inc. (PFG), CapEx growth YoY: 42.44%

Financials ETFs: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO)

data.title: Bed Bath & Beyond soars after earnings as analysts weigh in on the turnaround plan
data.description:
Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY) soared after issuing its first-quarter earnings report after the closing bell on Monday. The retailer is being sized up by analysts following the earnings update and recent M&A activity.

Wedbush Securities reiterated an Outperform rating on Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY) after taking in the report. Analyst Alicia Reese noted that the retailer is looking to turbocharge growth and margins by integrating and revitalizing its new home assets into a broader home solutions platform. "We expect at least $40 million in annualized cost savings and productivity efficiencies within 12–18 months of full integration," updated Reese. The firm has an $8.00 price target on BBBY. 

Needham is more cautious with a Hold rating on Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY). Analyst Bernie McTernan said the Q1 highlight for Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY) was revenue growing +6.9% Y/Y (+9.4% ex Canada) to mark the first positive Y/Y revenue growth in over 4 years. "We are adding Kirkland to our model, which helps revenue, but integration costs are a drag on '26E EBITDA. Our '27 estimates are roughly unchanged. Despite progress achieved by BBBY, we remain on the sidelines as there is still substantial work to be done to reach positive adj. EBITDA and successfully transition the core business from legacy e-commerce to the Everything Home," updated McTernan.

Jefferies also has a Hold rating on Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY). "Encouragingly, the core business returned to top-line growth while maintaining cost discipline. Management's ambitions for constructing an ecosystem to capture customer spend throughout the journey of homeowners are admirable, given prospects for cross-selling, optimized advertising, and attractive margin profiles of newer service-related businesses," updated Jonathan Matuszewski. However, the firm noted that it is early days in the BBBY portfolio approach, which introduces integration risk .

Shares of BBBY were up 24.9% in premarket action to $6.67, which is the highest trading level in more than three months. Short interest on BBBY is 11.4% of the total float.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Corning beats Q1 expectations, but guidance disappoints
Kiniksa Pharmaceuticals International, plc GAAP EPS of $0.27 beats by $0.09, revenue of $214.3M beats by $8.21M
data.description:
• Kiniksa Pharmaceuticals International, plc press release (KNSA): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.27 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $214.3M (+55.6% Y/Y) beats by $8.21M.
  • Kiniksa expects 2026 ARCALYST net product revenue of between $930 million and $945 million, compared to prior guidance of between $900 million and $920 million.
  • Kiniksa expects its current operating plan to remain cash flow positive on an annual basis.
